page: Amazon Web Services r8id.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. This server provides 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes. This local storage provides low-latency, high-speed data access directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with high marks in single-threaded gzip compression (34.32 GB/sec), bzip3 decompression (14.43 GB/sec), and Geekbench single-core testing (2169.0). However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks show notable limitations. The multi-core stress-ng performance and large-block memory read/write bandwidth fall into the lower percentiles, reflecting the constraints of a single physical core configuration. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput also align with entry-level multi-core capabilities.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast DDR5 memory, the r8id.large is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight caching layers, small-scale database hosting, and development environments requiring local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r8id.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX). Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, particularly in single-threaded compression and text processing, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are comparatively weak due to the single physical core design. This instance is best suited for memory-bound, single-threaded workloads, lightweight caching, and small database environments requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 118 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8id.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and 118 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and fast local NVMe SSD storage for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
118 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong single-core performance in compression and text processing
Supports AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Best suited for memory-bound, single-threaded applications and small databases
